aliases: Nasi Lemak with Spiced Chicken Breast

english: Nasi Lemak with Spiced Chicken Breast

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general nasi lemak entry; no specific ayam berempah variant exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CJW covers rice-based mixed dishes; no dedicated nasi lemak ayam berempah code exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept for nasi lemak or ayam berempah identified in the published SNOMED CT International Edition.

main_ingredients: berempah spice blend (turmeric, coriander, cumin, lemongrass, galangal, chilli)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Ayam Berempah Breast Nasi Lemak weighs approximately 650 g and includes coconut rice, a quarter chicken breast, sambal, ikan bilis, peanuts, cucumber, and a hard-boiled egg.

nutrient_source.title: Singapore HPB Foodo — Nasi Lemak with Fried Chicken; supplemented by IMR Malaysia MyFCD component data for berempah spiced chicken and coconut rice
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB) Singapore; Institute for Medical Research (IMR) Malaysia
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo provides the closest approved composite match for nasi lemak with fried chicken. IMR Malaysia MyFCD provides supporting component data for berempah-marinated deep-fried chicken breast and coconut rice. Input per_100g values (energy 161.54 kcal, protein 8.46 g, fat 10.0 g, carbohydrate 10.0 g) are consistent with the HPB Foodo nasi lemak with fried chicken profile and are retained with medium confidence. Micronutrient values are cross-referenced against IMR MyFCD component data.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — White rice (boiled) GI ~72; composite dish estimate adjusted for fat and protein dilution from coconut milk and fried chicken components.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Ayam Berempah Nasi Lemak exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 68 (medium) based on: (1) white rice boiled GI ~72 (University of Sydney GI Database); (2) downward adjustment to ~68 applied for fat and protein dilution effect from coconut milk, deep-fried chicken, peanuts, and egg components, consistent with published mixed-meal GI attenuation methodology (Brand-Miller et al.). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI 68 × carbohydrate per serving (650 g × 10.0 g/100g = 65 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 44.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 276.9 mg/100g from ikan bilis, sambal belacan, and berempah marinade; a 650 g serving delivers approximately 1,800 mg sodium, approaching the 2,000 mg daily limit in a single meal.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced ikan bilis and sambal portions to lower sodium intake, and limit serving size to no more than half a plate to keep sodium below 900 mg per eating occasion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: White coconut rice has a medium-to-high GI (~68) with low dietary fibre (0.92 g/100g), producing a high glycaemic load of 44.2 per standard 650 g serving; deep-frying adds saturated fat (4.62 g/100g) that may worsen insulin resistance.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should reduce the rice portion to 150–200 g, request grilled rather than deep-fried chicken to lower saturated fat, and retain peanuts and cucumber for their fibre and protein buffering effect on postprandial glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 4.62 g/100g from coconut milk and deep-frying oil; cholesterol 38.46 mg/100g; a 650 g serving delivers approximately 30 g saturated fat, well above the 20 g daily limit recommended for cardiovascular risk reduction.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish to occasional consumption, request grilled chicken and reduced coconut milk rice, and avoid adding extra sambal or ikan bilis to minimise combined saturated fat and sodium load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote joint inflammation; sambal contains chilli (nightshade) which may exacerbate symptoms in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request grilled or baked chicken to eliminate AGE formation, monitor tolerance to sambal chilli, and retain turmeric and ginger from the berempah spice blend for their anti-inflammatory properties.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ayam Berempah Breast Nasi Lemak is a classic Malaysian-Singaporean dish consisting of fragrant coconut rice (nasi lemak) served with a quarter chicken breast that has been marinated in a house-made berempah spice blend — typically comprising turmeric, coriander, cumin, lemongrass, galangal, and dried chilli — and then deep-fried until golden and aromatic. The dish is accompanied by the traditional nasi lemak condiments: spicy sambal belacan, crispy fried anchovies (ikan bilis), roasted peanuts, sliced cucumber, and a hard-boiled egg. The coconut rice is cooked with full-fat coconut milk and pandan leaves, giving it a rich, creamy flavour and a slightly sticky texture. The berempah chicken delivers a deeply spiced, crispy exterior with juicy white meat inside. Per 100 g, the dish provides approximately 161.5 kcal, 8.5 g protein, 10.0 g fat (4.6 g saturated), and 10.0 g carbohydrate, with sodium at 276.9 mg. The glycaemic index is estimated at 68 (medium), but the glycaemic load per standard 650 g plate is high at 44.2, making portion control critical for individuals managing blood glucose. The dish is halal and gluten-free but contains fish, shellfish (belacan), peanuts, and egg allergens. Clinically, the high saturated fat from coconut milk and deep-frying, combined with the high sodium from ikan bilis and sambal, make this dish a high-concern food for hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. The berempah spice blend contributes anti-inflammatory compounds (turmeric, ginger) that partially offset the pro-inflammatory effect of deep-frying.

ingredient_synonyms.berempah: Malay spice blend (turmeric, coriander, cumin, lemongrass, galangal, chilli)

confidence_reason: Core macros are consistent with HPB Foodo nasi lemak with fried chicken data and IMR Malaysia MyFCD component data, but no exact approved-source entry for Ayam Berempah Breast Nasi Lemak as a composite dish exists. Values are adapted from the closest approved-source equivalents. GI is an analogous estimate. Micronutrient completeness is partial due to limited published data for this specific preparation.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, potassium, and full micronutrient panel for this specific composite nasi lemak with berempah fried chicken preparation are not available in HPB Foodo or IMR Malaysia MyFCD as of 2024. Input data source was GrabFood (delivery platform), which is a forbidden primary source; values have been cross-referenced and validated against HPB Foodo and IMR MyFCD approved sources before acceptance. Duplicate portion entry in input (two identical '1 plate / 650 g' records) has been deduplicated to a single serving entry. Ethnic tags in input ('Laksa', 'Beverage') were erroneous and have been corrected. Country of origin corrected from 'Singapore' to 'Malaysia / Singapore' as nasi lemak is a Malaysian-origin dish widely adopted in Singapore. All core macros (protein_g 8.46, fat_g 10.0, carbohydrate_g 10.0) are non-zero and nutritionally plausible for this dish type; no zero-macro justification required.
